metaTitle: Sheng Ji vs Traditional Feng Shui: Living Tomb vs Ancestral Burial — Key Differences
metaDescription: Sheng Ji (living tomb) benefits the living person directly in 1–6 months. Traditional Yin Zhai Feng Shui benefits descendants over generations. Both use dragon veins but serve different purposes. WeChat NSDG521.
metaKeywords: Sheng Ji vs Feng Shui, living tomb vs ancestral burial, Sheng Ji difference, Yin Zhai Feng Shui vs Sheng Ji, life base vs traditional burial
h1: Sheng Ji vs Traditional Feng Shui (Yin Zhai): 7 Key Differences Between Living Tomb and Ancestral Burial
heroSub: Both draw upon dragon vein earth qi — but the beneficiary, purpose, timeline and mechanism are fundamentally different

items.h3: Sheng Ji: Dragon Vein Energy for a Living Person's Own Destiny
items.content: Sheng Ji (生基) places a living person's life information — hair, nail clippings, blood, garment — in a genuine dragon vein plot while the person is still alive. The dragon vein's earth qi bonds with the living client and continuously elevates their destiny in real time. The client themselves is the beneficiary, and effects begin manifesting within months of the ceremony.

items.h3: Yin Zhai Feng Shui: Dragon Vein Energy Transmitted to Descendants via Ancestors
items.content: Yin Zhai Feng Shui (陰宅風水) involves selecting a propitious burial site for a deceased ancestor. The earth qi of the burial location transmits its energy to the ancestor's descendants through the physical remains, benefiting their career, wealth, marriage and fertility across generations. The beneficiaries are the descendants, not the deceased person.
Sheng Ji: the living client benefits directly and personally. Yin Zhai: the deceased person's descendants benefit — the interred individual is already beyond receiving benefit themselves.

Sheng Ji: effects typically begin within 1 to 6 months of the ceremony — facial complexion improvement first, then improved interpersonal relationships, then concrete career and financial changes. Yin Zhai: effects typically require years to decades to manifest, and may only become apparent in the next generation or later.

items.h3: Difference 3: What Is Placed in the Earth
items.content: Sheng Ji: four living items — hair, nail clippings, blood-infused paper, and a worn garment — along with a paper effigy, are interred. Yin Zhai: the physical remains (bones or ashes) of the deceased are interred; the earth qi transmits through the ancestral DNA to living descendants.

Sheng Ji requires a fully ordained Taoist master (with documented lineage) to perform a complete 2-day ceremony including prayers, disaster-dispelling, wealth rituals and empowerment rites. Yin Zhai Feng Shui primarily requires an expert Feng Shui geomancer (地師) skilled in site selection and auspicious date calculation — the ritual elements are comparatively simpler.

items.h3: Differences 5–7: Dragon Vein Type, Impact on Descendants, Duration
items.content: Dragon vein type: Sheng Ji requires a Yang-natured plot with active, upwelling qi; Yin Zhai requires a stable Yin-natured plot with deep, settled qi — not all plots are suitable for both purposes. Impact on descendants: Sheng Ji's benefits are exclusively personal to the client and do not affect descendants positively or negatively. Yin Zhai is specifically designed to benefit descendants. Duration: Sheng Ji has defined time limits (5-year or Lifetime package). Yin Zhai effects theoretically persist across multiple generations as long as the burial site remains undisturbed.

items.h3: Choose Sheng Ji When You Need Personal Destiny Improvement Now
items.content: If your goal is to improve your own career, wealth, health or relationships — and you want to see meaningful changes within your own lifetime — Sheng Ji is the right choice. It is the most direct and fastest-acting method for personal destiny elevation within the Taoist Feng Shui tradition.

items.h3: Can You Do Both Sheng Ji and Yin Zhai Feng Shui?
items.content: Yes — the two practices operate on different levels and do not interfere with each other. A family seeking comprehensive destiny management might pursue Sheng Ji for the living members who need personal improvement, while also ensuring that recently deceased ancestors are given propitious Yin Zhai burial sites to benefit future generations. Contact Mr. Liang Dongrong (WeChat: NSDG521) to discuss your specific situation.
